items.q: Which verb is used to say 'I am happy today' (temporary state)?
items.why: Students treat ser and estar as identical. SER for permanent traits, identity, time, origin. ESTAR for temporary states, location, ongoing actions. 'Soy contento' would suggest 'I am a happy person', not 'I'm happy right now'.

items.correct: Could be his/her/your(formal)/their brother - context determines which.
items.why: Students assume su = his. SU is one of Spanish's most context-dependent words. Means his, her, your (usted/ustedes), or their. Speakers often clarify with 'de él', 'de ella', 'de usted' if ambiguous: 'el hermano de él'.

items.q: How do you say 'I am going to visit Spain' in Spanish?
items.why: Students drop the 'a' or use a conjugated verb. The near future is IR (conjugated) + A + INFINITIVE. The 'a' is obligatory: voy A comer, vamos A ver, vas A estudiar. Heavily tested for holiday/future plans.
Correct answer: diversas. “diverso” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which country declared itself plurinational in 2009?
items.why: Correct answer: Bolivia. “Ecuador” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Roughly how many million speak Quechua (nearest 1)?
items.why: The real figure is 8 million. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
